title: Resnik — Failing Faith (1986)
description: Judith Resnik — Failing Faith: Adjudicatory Procedure in Decline (53 U. Chi. L. Rev. 494 (1986))
show_notes:
Judith Resnik — Failing Faith: Adjudicatory Procedure in Decline (53 U. Chi. L. Rev. 494 (1986))

In this companion to Managerial Judges, Judith Resnik charts the decline of adjudication as the animating ideal of American civil procedure. She traces how the faith embodied in the 1938 Federal Rules — in trans-substantive procedure, liberal access, and the trial as the system's centerpiece — has eroded under caseload pressure, managerial judging, settlement, and privatized dispute resolution, and asks what is lost as courts turn away from adjudication.
